sensors: 4 stereo vision cameras, RGB camera, VSLAM
notes: Marked HOT on Keenon site. 9 facial expressions, movable head, customizable accessories.
Marked NEW on Keenon site. Best for tight-space restaurants.
T9 Pro variant also available with enhanced VSLAM.

sensors: 2+1 binocular vision (204-degree), obstacle detection down to 5cm
notes: Marked HOT. Price varies significantly by market/distributor.
Best for privacy-focused delivery (hotels, hospitals).
Primary hotel robot. Elevator integration via E-Box kit (sold separately). FCC Part 15 Class B compliant.
Elevator integration via IoT kit (shared with W3 and S100). Best for premium surfaces.

navigation_method: LiDAR + stereo vision + ultrasonic + line lasers
sensors: 10 detection components: LiDAR, stereo vision, ultrasonic, line lasers
notes: Marked NEW. Fastest charging in Keenon cleaning lineup.
Largest in KLEENBOT lineup. 60L clean + 47L waste tanks.
Best for small commercial spaces.

sensors: LiDAR, stereo vision, low-hanging object detection
notes: Elevator integration via IoT kit (shared with W3 and C30).
Marked NEW. Highest payload in Keenon lineup.
Pilot at Shangri-La Traders Hotel. CES 2026 showcase. Wheeled base, not bipedal.
Debuted WAIC 2025. Pilot partners: Shangri-La, Walmart.

sensors: 150-degree RGB camera, LiDAR, rain sensor, drop-off detection
notes: First Keenon consumer product. Kickstarter April 2026, retail late May 2026.
120,000+ units shipped globally across all Pudu models.

sensors: 3D multi-sensor fusion, detects obstacles as low as 3cm
notes: Purpose-built for multi-floor buildings. 2-4 adjustable compartments.

sensors: rear AI camera for cleaning quality detection
key_differentiators: world's first rear AI camera for cleaning quality
notes: Launched 2025. ASEAN shipments began Jan 2026.
Indoor, semi-outdoor, and outdoor capable. 35L trash bin. 60Ah battery.
Launched iREX 2025. D5-W variant adds wheels for longer runtime.
Flagship product. 581-688 kg depending on config. No elevator integration.
Purpose-built for spaces too tight for Neo.

sensors: 2D/3D LiDAR, sonar arrays, bumper sensors, cliff sensors
fleet_management: TUG Mission Control (cloud dispatch, priority queuing)
notes: Acquired by ST Engineering 2016. Pharmacy variant has biometric access, controlled substance security.

sensors: 2D/3D LiDAR, RGB-D cameras, force/torque on arm
key_differentiators: only hospital robot with 7-DOF manipulation arm
notes: RaaS only. Deployed at Texas Health, Cedars-Sinai. Tiger Global-backed.
Most mature commercial quadruped. 564 Wh battery.

sensors: vision system, suction array end effector
notes: Customers: DHL, Maersk, Gap, H&M, NFI.

sensors: dual 8.85MP 90Hz stereo fisheye cameras, 4 beamforming mics
notes: Pre-order with $200 deposit. US deliveries 2026. Most tasks still teleoperated as of late 2025.

navigation_method: dual LiDAR + cameras + UWB, pre-scanned maps
notes: Deployed: LAX, MIA, SEA, Schiphol, Haneda, Pearson. 3+ hospitals in Japan.

sensors: laser scanners, 3D cameras, heat sensors, motion sensors
key_differentiators: first-mover in autonomous UV-C hospital disinfection
notes: Not registered as medical device. BS 8628:2022 compliant. Parent company ISO 13485 certified.
Directly relevant to SPHM workflows. Reduces caregiver strain injuries.

sensors: torque sensors on all 7 axes (+/-2% accuracy)
key_differentiators: only IEC 60601-1 certified robot arm for medical integration
notes: Sold as OEM component to medical device manufacturers. LBR Med 14 variant: 14 kg payload, 820mm reach.

sensors: laser scanner, two 3D cameras, QR camera
notes: Part of KMP family (250P/600P/1500P/3000P). Shares accessories and charging infrastructure.
Upgraded BellaBot with triple-fusion navigation (VSLAM+Marker+LiDAR).
Marketing/reception robot with large touchscreen display and delivery capability.
Long-runtime delivery robot with PUDU's proprietary VSLAM+ navigation.

key_differentiators: 60 kg highest payload in Pudu delivery line
notes: Highest payload (60 kg) in Pudu delivery line. Enclosed cabinet for collecting dirty dishes.
Base model of CC1 line. Pricing confirmed via RobotLAB distributor.
Sweeper-only model. Extremely high coverage rate for large facilities.

key_differentiators: combined sweep and vacuum in one pass
notes: MT1 variant with added vacuum capability. Lower coverage rate than sweep-only MT1 due to vacuum power draw.
Large-format scrubber for warehouse/airport scale. Pro variant has enhanced capabilities.
Niche product — automated escalator handrail disinfection. Limited public specs.

sensors: LiDAR, depth cameras, arm force/torque sensors
notes: Early/limited commercial availability. Dual 7-DOF arms on mobile base.
Announced semi-humanoid robot. 30-50 DOF. Not yet commercially available.
Research-stage bipedal humanoid. 42 DOF. Not for sale.
ISO 3691-4 certified industrial AMR. Entry-level payload in Pudu industrial line.
CE-MD certified industrial AMR. Mid-range payload.
One of only two Pudu models with VDA 5050 support. Flagship industrial AMR.
T600 variant with underride mechanism for docking under carts/shelves. Also VDA 5050 compatible.

sensors: LiDAR, RGBD camera, ToF sensor, AI object detection
key_differentiators: AI-powered navigation with multiple sensor fusion
notes: DISCONTINUED in US market. LG redirecting to Bear Robotics partnership. Still available in some Asian markets.
Enclosed compartment variant for hotel delivery. Limited availability. 30 kg per compartment.
Visitor guidance robot. Limited availability. LG product line in active transition.
UV-C disinfection robot. 15-30 min per room. Availability unclear — LG product line in transition.

sensors: Multi-camera array, LiDAR, GPS, IMU, ultrasonic
key_differentiators: Uber Eats exclusive US robot partner
notes: Outdoor-only sidewalk delivery robot. Exclusive Uber Eats US robot delivery partner. Publicly traded (NASDAQ: SERV).

sensors: Cameras, LiDAR, GPS, neural net perception
notes: Outdoor-only sidewalk delivery. 1,000+ robots deployed. Partnerships with Uber Eats, DoorDash, Wolt.
Warehouse variant of the Neo. Same scrubbing capability but optimized for distribution center environments.
Compact 4-in-1 cleaner. May be Gaussian Robotics / third-party platform sold under Navia brand.
Mid-size commercial scrubber. 3-8h runtime. Distributed by Navia in US market.
Large-format scrubber for high-volume facilities. 4-6h runtime. Distributed by Navia.

key_differentiators: lowest-cost delivery robot with known pricing
notes: Only robot on Navia's site with published MSRP ($11,999) and lease rate ($700/mo). Budget delivery option.

key_differentiators: 200 kg AMR base + 11-15 kg cobot arm
notes: Mobile cobot platform: AMR base (200 kg) with LBR iisy collaborative arm (11-15 kg payload).

sensors: Laser scanners, 7-axis torque sensors on arm
key_differentiators: 200 kg AMR + 7-14 kg sensitive arm with torque sensors
notes: Mobile platform with LBR iiwa sensitive arm. 7-axis torque sensing for precision tasks.
Heaviest mobile manipulator. 7-ton platform with QUANTEC industrial arm (150 kg payload).
Newest addition to KMP platform line. Available since December 2025.
Heavy-payload platform AMR. VDA 5050 compliant.
Heaviest pure platform AMR in KUKA lineup. 3-ton payload capacity.

key_differentiators: basis for KMR iiwa mobile platform
notes: KUKA's flagship collaborative arm. 7-axis torque sensing. 7 kg and 14 kg payload variants.

key_differentiators: LiDAR natural feature nav — no infrastructure changes
notes: Automated counterbalance stacker. 4,000 lb capacity. Navigates via natural features — no floor markers.
Automated tug for cart towing. 2,200 lb tow capacity. 1.9 mph max speed.
Compact light-duty AGV. 200 kg payload. LiDAR natural feature navigation.
Budget AGV. Magnetic tape guided (not autonomous SLAM). Lowest-cost option in Toyota AGV line.

sensors: LiDAR or magnetic tape, safety scanners
notes: Automated tow tractor for pulling cart trains. Multiple navigation options.
Underfloor transport system. Vehicles move under floor level — unique approach for space-constrained facilities.
Not commercially available to general market. Currently deployed at BMW manufacturing only. $1B+ Series C.
Announced only. No specs available. Successor to Figure 02.
Internal factory use only. Not commercially available. $30K target price stated by Elon Musk. 73 kg weight, 20 kg payload.
Wheeled humanoid for enterprise security. Charter program only. Predecessor to bipedal NEO.
All-electric successor to hydraulic Atlas. 56 DOF. Early adopter program with Hyundai. Not yet generally available.
Consumer power wheelchair. Not autonomous. 136 kg (300 lbs) user weight capacity.
Foldable travel wheelchair. 24 kg. Not autonomous.
Mobility scooter. Not autonomous. 5 mph, 10.7 mile range.
Swiss autonomous wheelchair startup. Early commercial stage. Zurich and Schiphol airport pilots. Key differentiator vs WHILL: true omni-directional movement.
Pharma-specific variant of UVD robot. Pharmaceutical compliance features.

key_differentiators: Blue Ocean Robot Venture Factory product
notes: Telepresence robot. 8h battery. Primarily remote-controlled with some autonomous features.
Pudu's disinfection robot. Combined UV-C and chemical dry mist disinfection.
